pattern: A recursive grammar evaluation where comma means set union and adjacency means cartesian-product concatenation, returning the sorted distinct words.
hints: Two operations build the answer: a comma joins alternatives into one set, and writing expressions side by side concatenates every pair across them.
Parse the top level into comma-separated groups, and within a group split into concatenated factors, recursing into each braced factor.
Evaluate each factor to a set; concatenate factors of a group by cartesian product, union the groups, then return the unique words sorted.

edge_inputs.description: first example, concatenation over a nested union
nested union producing the same set twice

stress.description: randomly built valid grammar expressions of bounded size
stress.note: keeps expressions within the 60-char limit and grammatically valid
